goal: Build a product-photography key/fill/rim 3-light rig plus HDRI around the hero robot for a trade-show reel.
thoughts: HDRI first for grounding reflections, then three-point rig layered on top. Warm key, cooler fill at lower intensity, cooler-still rim behind. Do not touch PhysX or articulation -- look-dev is pure rendering. Post-process tonemap/bloom is a separate pass so she can A/B without baking decisions in.
code:
create_hdri_skydome(
    path="/World/Lighting/SkyDome",
    texture_path="<HDRI_EXR>",
    intensity="<FLOAT>",
    rotation="<DEGREES>"
)
for role, pos, color_k, inten in [
    ("Key",  "<POS>", 5500, "<HIGH>"),
    ("Fill", "<POS>", 7000, "<MED>"),
    ("Rim",  "<POS>", 8000, "<MED>")
]:
    p = f"/World/Lighting/{role}"
    create_prim(path=p, type_name="RectLight", translate=pos)
    set_light_color(prim_path=p, temperature_k=color_k)
    set_light_intensity(prim_path=p, intensity=inten)
enable_post_process(
    tonemap="ACES",
    exposure="<EV>",
    bloom_enabled=True,
    bloom_intensity="<FLOAT>"
)
capture_viewport(path="<OUT_PNG>", width=1920, height=1080)
failure_modes: Recommending Arnold-only workflow when RTX Interactive can hold up for stills
Confusing HDRI intensity with dome rotation when highlights clip
Touching articulation or PhysX for a pure look-dev task
